mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-08-15 indicates sustained low-rate regime
regime_hint: post-crisis monetary accommodation with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market tightening and rising consumer purchasing power, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,598 thousand as of 2016-08-15 indicates near-full employment trajectory
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth > 150k are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal triggers in fewer than 40% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously signals weakening consumer demand and housing investment, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 456,321 million and HOUST at 1,211 thousand as of 2016-08-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month, or joint signal has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and falling manufacturing capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.3% are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.2%, t-test p > 0.15), or signal triggers in fewer than 35% of months.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens discretionary spending and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as forward earnings guidance declines.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-08-15 is near the trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 5.0% are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.5%, t-test p > 0.10), or the signal fails to predict drawdowns in at least 50% of recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust aggregate demand expansion and rising corporate revenue growth, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,570.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-04-15
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 > 2.5% growth are not significantly positive (mean return < 2.0%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL month-over-month > 0.3%) signals financial repression that erodes real returns on bonds, forcing investors into equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% and CPIAUCSL at 239.828 as of 2016-08-15
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both conditions are met, or joint signal has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,100 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, which predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as construction-related earnings and consumer wealth effects deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,211 thousand as of 2016-08-15 is above trigger but near threshold
regime_hint: housing market slowdown or demand-side contraction
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 1,100 are not significantly negative (mean return > 0.3%, t-test p > 0.15), or signal triggers in fewer than 30% of months with housing weakness.
